name: Barkley's 5 Executive Function Deficit Domains
citation: Barkley RA. Executive Functions: What They Are, How They Work, and Why They Evolved. Guilford Press, 2012.

self_management_time.label: Self-Management to Time
self_management_time.description: Ability to organise behaviour across time, estimate durations, meet deadlines, and manage schedules.
self_management_time.adhd_manifestation: Time-blindness, chronic lateness, underestimating task duration, missing deadlines.

self_organization.label: Self-Organisation & Problem Solving
self_organization.description: Ability to plan, organise materials and actions, and solve problems sequentially.
self_organization.adhd_manifestation: Cluttered workspace, forgotten steps, difficulty starting multi-step tasks.

self_restraint.label: Self-Restraint (Inhibition)
self_restraint.description: Ability to inhibit prepotent responses, resist distractions, and delay gratification.
self_restraint.adhd_manifestation: Impulsive app switching, distraction spirals, difficulty resisting social media.

self_motivation.label: Self-Motivation
self_motivation.description: Ability to sustain effort and motivation without external reinforcement.
self_motivation.adhd_manifestation: Task avoidance, procrastination, difficulty starting boring but necessary work.

self_regulation_emotion.label: Self-Regulation of Emotion
self_regulation_emotion.description: Ability to modulate emotional responses, especially frustration, anger, and excitement.
self_regulation_emotion.adhd_manifestation: Emotional flooding, frustration spirals, rejection sensitive dysphoria (RSD), difficulty calming down.
